articles.title: NASCAR Gets a New CEO: Steve O'Donnell Promises to 'Unite' and Bring Back the Fun
articles.summary: Steve O'Donnell has been named CEO of NASCAR, the first person outside the France family to hold the top job. He vows to unite the industry and restore the fun, calling NASCAR a 'badass American sport.' The previous CEO, Jim France, stepped down after a contentious antitrust lawsuit and a trial that revealed embarrassing texts. O'Donnell aims to listen to all stakeholders and address issues with urgency.

articles.title: Ticks Are Out in Force: Doctors Warn of a Potentially Severe Season for Lyme Disease
articles.summary: U.S. doctors are warning that tick-borne diseases like Lyme disease could surge this year. CDC data shows ER visits for tick bites are at their highest for this time of year since 2017. Factors include a mild winter, high mouse populations, and climate change. Lyme disease affects about 476,000 people annually. Experts advise using insect repellent, wearing light clothing, and checking for ticks after outdoor activities.

articles.title: Rocky Statue Finally Enters the Philadelphia Museum of Art — 50 Years After the Film
articles.summary: The iconic bronze statue of Rocky Balboa has been moved inside the Philadelphia Museum of Art for the first time, as part of an exhibition marking the 50th anniversary of the original film. The statue, which stood outside for over 20 years, is now featured alongside more than 150 artworks and ancient artifacts. The exhibition, 'Rising Up,' explores how monuments are made and what they mean.
